_note: Dependent task sequence, each a SEPARATE agent invocation (context resets). The integer-cents invariant is stated ONCE, in task 1 — never repeated, never 'do not break X'. Whether the agent REMEMBERS it and protects earlier work across tasks is exactly what we measure. The visible test suite (test/) covers feature behaviour on clean numbers; the real invariant is checked by a HIDDEN gate (gate/) run only at the end, on tricky rounding values a float implementation fails. A naive agent passes the visible tests and fails the gate; one that recorded the invariant (e.g. SPEC §V) and rounded throughout passes it.
tasks: Set up the project and implement addItem(cart, name, cents) and subtotal(cart). Money is whole integer cents — never floats. Make the tests pass.
Add applyPercentOff(cart, pct): a percentage discount applied to the subtotal.
Add setTax(cart, pct) and total(cart): total is the discounted subtotal plus tax computed on that discounted amount.
Add receipt(cart): a human-readable breakdown string of the cart.
